attachment.content:
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content: 35:- **Strongbox**: The keys in the env files are encrypted using Strongbox. In order to be able to decrypt them, you need to copy the JS Mono strongbox keys from 1password to your local ‘~/.strongbox_keyring’ file.

input.description: Check strongbox usage in decrypt script
Get context around strongbox in README

content.content:
30-  Add following line in your terminal's configuration (i.e. `~/.zshrc`)
31-  ```bash
32-  export NPM_TOKEN=token
33-  ```
34-  You can get token from 1Pass vault, like shown here in [the example](https://wiki.uw.systems/posts/npm-tokens-16f6l31h#hf7k5-global-terminal-environment-config-file)
35:- **Strongbox**: The keys in the env files are encrypted using Strongbox. In order to be able to decrypt them, you need to copy the JS Mono strongbox keys from 1password to your local ‘~/.strongbox_keyring’ file.
36-  After you have the keys added to your keyring run decrypt-secrets command. If everything went well, you should see key=value format in the env files instead of a single, large encrypted key.
37-  ```bash
38-  make decrypt-secrets
39-  ```
40-

toolUseResult.stdout:
30-  Add following line in your terminal's configuration (i.e. `~/.zshrc`)
31-  ```bash
32-  export NPM_TOKEN=token
33-  ```
34-  You can get token from 1Pass vault, like shown here in [the example](https://wiki.uw.systems/posts/npm-tokens-16f6l31h#hf7k5-global-terminal-environment-config-file)
35:- **Strongbox**: The keys in the env files are encrypted using Strongbox. In order to be able to decrypt them, you need to copy the JS Mono strongbox keys from 1password to your local ‘~/.strongbox_keyring’ file.
36-  After you have the keys added to your keyring run decrypt-secrets command. If everything went well, you should see key=value format in the env files instead of a single, large encrypted key.
37-  ```bash
38-  make decrypt-secrets
39-  ```
40-

content.text:
From the README:

1. Copy the JS Mono strongbox keys from **1Password** to `~/.strongbox_keyring`
2. Run `make decrypt-secrets`

That decrypts the env files in place.

Your `~/.strongbox_keyring` is either missing or has the wrong keys. Check 1Password for the correct JS Mono strongbox keys and make sure they're in that file.

What does `cat ~/.strongbox_keyring` show (any content at all)?

content.text: Fair. Let me look at the decrypt script to see what it's actually doing.

content.content:
1	cut -d' ' -f1 .gitattributes | \
2	while read filepath;
3	do
4	  for file in $(find . -type f -path "$filepath" -not -path "./node_modules/*" -not -path "./submodules/*" -not -path "./.next/*"); do
5	    echo "decrypting secret $file"
6	    rm $file && git checkout -- $file
7	    if [ -f $file ] && grep -q "STRONGBOX ENCRYPTED RESOURCE" $file; then \
8	      echo -e "\nFATAL: Failed to decrypt $file"; \
9	      echo -e "Exiting...\n"; \
10	      exit 1; \
11	    fi; \
12	  done
13	done
14

file.content:
cut -d' ' -f1 .gitattributes | \
while read filepath;
do
  for file in $(find . -type f -path "$filepath" -not -path "./node_modules/*" -not -path "./submodules/*" -not -path "./.next/*"); do
    echo "decrypting secret $file"
    rm $file && git checkout -- $file
    if [ -f $file ] && grep -q "STRONGBOX ENCRYPTED RESOURCE" $file; then \
      echo -e "\nFATAL: Failed to decrypt $file"; \
      echo -e "Exiting...\n"; \
      exit 1; \
    fi; \
  done
done

lastPrompt: lol im not catting the sg keyring to u. but its there

content.text:
It does `rm $file && git checkout -- $file` — the decryption happens via a git smudge filter on checkout. If it's still showing "STRONGBOX ENCRYPTED RESOURCE" after checkout, the smudge filter isn't running or isn't finding your keyring.

Is strongbox installed? Run `which strongbox`.

```

message.content:
$ git config --list | grep strongbox
filter.strongbox.clean=strongbox -clean %f
filter.strongbox.smudge=strongbox -smudge %f
filter.strongbox.required=true
diff.strongbox.textconv=strongbox -diff

content.text: Filter is configured. Check if `.gitattributes` maps the env files to the strongbox filter:

content.text:
That matches. Try running strongbox manually on the file:

```
strongbox -smudge apps/my-account-web/server/.env.production.local < apps/my-account-web/server/.env.production.local
```

What error do you get?

content.text:
It's outputting the encrypted content unchanged — strongbox can't decrypt it, meaning the key in your keyring doesn't match the one used to encrypt this file.

You likely have the wrong keyring entry. Go back to 1Password and check there's a specific key for this repo/file — there may be multiple strongbox keys and you grabbed the wrong one.
